statement.markdown: You are given a queue class with enqueue and dequeue operations. The dequeue method should remove and return the front element of the queue. However, the current dequeue method has a logical bug that needs to be fixed.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be provided as a series of commands to enqueue and dequeue elements. The commands will be separated by newline characters.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the elements dequeued from the queue.
statement.notes: The queue should support both enqueue and dequeue operations, and the dequeue operation should return the front element of the queue.

examples.explanation: The input represents three enqueue operations followed by three dequeue operations. The expected output are the elements dequeued from the queue.

hints.text: Check the dequeue method for the logical bug.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The dequeue method should remove and return the front element of the queue.
editorial.bruteForce: Implement a brute force solution by checking each element in the queue.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a pointer or index to keep track of the front of the queue.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Demonstrate that the dequeue method correctly removes and returns the front element of the queue.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run to illustrate the dequeue method.
